mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near zero and CPIAUCSL is still rising, real policy rates stay deeply negative and equities reprice toward lower discount rates. This predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as long as inflation does not collapse and rate cuts remain constrained.
failure_condition: Rejected if the triggered sample shows negative median 20d SPX returns or if positive returns do not exceed the unconditional baseline by at least 5 percentage points over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE with falling PAYEMS marks weakening household income and slower consumption, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when payroll losses are broad rather than a one-month noise spike.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if unemployment-threshold events are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if PAYEMS fails to decline around the trigger in the same episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST indicates housing-cycle contraction, which reduces construction demand, household wealth effects, and cyclical earnings momentum. That shock should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, especially when housing starts remain below recent trend.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Rejected if HOUST drawdown triggers are not followed by below-baseline 60d SPX returns or if the sign of the effect turns positive for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with stable CPIAUCSL signals supply-side normalization: firms are producing more without a matching inflation surge, which supports margins and risk appetite. This should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when output growth is not offset by accelerating prices.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average while CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change stays below 3%
failure_condition: Rejected if positive INDPRO/CPI combinations fail to outperform unconditional 20d SPX returns by at least 3 percentage points, or if the sign is negative across two distinct expansionary subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue growth for consumer-facing firms, which should lift equity prices over the following month. The effect should weaken when inflation is accelerating because nominal sales gains then reflect prices rather than real demand.
failure_condition: Rejected if rising RSAFS episodes do not produce positive excess 20d SPX returns, or if the sign remains unchanged after conditioning on inflation acceleration versus deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% signals restrictive policy that raises discount rates and tightens credit conditions, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal is strongest when rate policy is actively above neutral rather than merely stable.
failure_condition: Rejected if high-FEDFUNDS observations are followed by positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the short-horizon hit rate falls below 45% in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is falling while UNRATE is rising, the labor market is contracting in a way that directly weakens household income and corporate sales expectations. That combination should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, not just a short-lived one-week response.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises over the same span
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint labor-stress trigger does not lead to negative 60d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the effect is fully explained by either PAYEMS or UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad nominal demand acceleration, which should lift earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when GDP improves but retail sales remain flat, because then the growth impulse is too narrow to reprice equities.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior quarter/month comparison
failure_condition: Rejected if simultaneous GDPC1/RSAFS upturns fail to produce positive 60d SPX returns above baseline, or if GDP-only improvements outperform the joint condition.
